name: How do I handle moisture and mold in a poorly vented coastal bathroom before remodeling?

mainEntity.name: If my existing bathroom in Pacifica or Half Moon Bay already has mold and peeling paint from poor ventilation, what should be done during the remodel to fully solve the problem instead of just covering it up?
mainEntity.text: If my existing bathroom in Pacifica or Half Moon Bay already has mold and peeling paint from poor ventilation, what should be done during the remodel to fully solve the problem instead of just covering it up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Existing mold and peeling paint in a Pacifica or Half Moon Bay bathroom signal that ventilation and moisture control have been inadequate. A cosmetic remodel that simply covers surfaces will not solve the problem and may hide ongoing damage. During demolition, ask your contractor to fully remove mold‑stained drywall, compromised insulation, and any soft or rotten subfloor. Mold‑affected framing should be cleaned, dried, and treated with appropriate products before being closed up. Wet areas like showers should be rebuilt with a continuous waterproofing system behind tile, not just tile over standard drywall. Upgrading ventilation is essential. Your contractor should calculate the correct exhaust fan capacity based on bathroom size and duct length, use smooth ducting where possible, and vent fully to the exterior, not into the attic or crawlspace. Consider a fan with a humidity sensor so it runs long enough to clear moisture after showers. Air sealing and insulation in exterior walls and around windows can reduce condensation, especially in foggy conditions. High‑quality, mold‑resistant paint and properly installed caulk help finish the job. Have your contractor explain how each part of the remodel—demolition, waterproofing, ventilation, and finishes—works together to eliminate the root cause of your previous mold issues rather than just hiding the symptoms.
acceptedAnswer.description: A moldy coastal bathroom needs more than new finishes. During the remodel, remove damaged materials, treat any mold, add proper waterproofing in wet areas, and upgrade to a correctly sized exhaust fan vented outdoors. Improve air sealing and insulation where appropriate so humidity can be controlled instead of trapped behind new tile and paint.
